prompt: A listener asked whether Eric’s switch to a fully vegan diet changed his training, performance, recovery, or supplementation approach.
expected_output: Eric said he noticed no meaningful performance or recovery change after going vegan. Training remained broadly the same, with recent increases in training effort due to feeling good overall. His main nutrition adjustment was paying more attention to protein-source diversity (pea, rice, and soy) to improve amino acid coverage. He continued algae oil (EPA/DHA source) and a multivitamin, with B12 coverage noted as important. Greg added that iron deficiency is generally less common in men, while still acknowledging context can matter (e.g., high running volume).
assertions: Answer states Eric noticed no meaningful performance or recovery change after going vegan
Answer mentions protein-source diversity (pea, rice, soy) for amino acid coverage
Answer includes algae oil for EPA/DHA and B12 as important supplements
Answer does NOT claim vegan diet is superior or inferior for performance
